status: exact corrected C1 transversality reduction for positive Newman collisions; not a proof of the lower bound, Lambda<=0, or RH
proof_boundary: This artifact proves the normalized double-zero equivalence, the corrected finite first-jet identity, and the exact C1 exclusion lemma. It does not prove uniform first-jet transversality, supply complete refined remainder constants, close the compact region, exclude a positive Newman boundary, prove Lambda<=0, or prove RH.

exact.double_zero: H_t=H_t'=0 if and only if Z_t=Z_t'=0
exact.strict_target: T_L[J]>epsilon_0^2+epsilon_1^2 uniformly on the critical region
exact.cauchy_gain: A radius-1/L collar transfers the refined scalar remainder through one derivative with one factor L; no second-derivative remainder is needed
exact.global_composition: Combine the oscillatory-zeta theorem for every fixed tL>=c_*+epsilon at sufficiently large L, the critical transversality target on the residual high-frequency layer 0<tL<=c_*+o(1), and compact no-double-zero certificates for every bounded-L remainder, where c_*=4911678521/1933561194
exact.conditional_endgame: If all three regions are closed, positive-boundary attainment gives Lambda<=0, hence RH

rows.claim: The positive normalizer preserves the value-and-first-derivative double-zero condition.
rows.formula: H_t=A_t*Z_t with A_t>0; H_t'=A_t*((log A_t)'*Z_t+Z_t'); H_t=H_t'=0 if and only if Z_t=Z_t'=0
rows.proof_boundary: Exact on the real axis because A_t is strictly positive.

rows.claim: The refined Polymath-15 approximation splits the normalized heat flow into a corrected main and a smaller remainder.
rows.proof_boundary: Uses the published A+B-C approximation with fixed cutoff on a collar.

rows.claim: The corrected finite value and first derivative have an exact two-component formula.
rows.proof_boundary: No division by D and valid when the complex finite sum vanishes.

rows.claim: Distance of the corrected main from a double zero is a positive sum of two squares.
rows.proof_boundary: L=log(x/(4*pi))>0 in the Polymath high-frequency region.

rows.claim: A strict corrected-main first-jet lower bound excludes an exact double zero.
rows.proof_boundary: Requires rigorous normalized C1 remainder caps, including cutoff collars.

rows.claim: The collision target saves one Cauchy derivative compared with global Laguerre-curvature transfer.
rows.formula: A radius-1/L collar transfers the refined scalar remainder through one derivative with one factor L; no second-derivative remainder is needed
rows.proof_boundary: The scalar refined remainder still needs explicit uniform constants.

rows.claim: The critical asymptotic problem is reduced to corrected finite first-jet transversality.
rows.formula: T_L[J]>epsilon_0^2+epsilon_1^2 uniformly on the critical region
rows.proof_boundary: Open and RH-level; no uniform lower bound is asserted.

rows.claim: The no-collision endgame separates into the proved outer asymptotic region, residual critical high frequency, and bounded-L certificate regions.
rows.formula: Combine the oscillatory-zeta theorem for every fixed tL>=c_*+epsilon at sufficiently large L, the critical transversality target on the residual high-frequency layer 0<tL<=c_*+o(1), and compact no-double-zero certificates for every bounded-L remainder, where c_*=4911678521/1933561194
rows.proof_boundary: The residual critical and bounded-L regions remain open; the outer theorem has an existential, not numerical, L_epsilon.

rows.claim: Closing the three-region no-double-zero partition would prove the missing Newman direction.
rows.formula: If all three regions are closed, positive-boundary attainment gives Lambda<=0, hence RH
rows.proof_boundary: Conditional on the open critical and compact certificates.

rows.claim: Positive point scouts and the exact sum-of-squares identity do not prove the strict lower bound.
